title: AtlasPI: A structured historical geographic database for AI agents
description:
AtlasPI is an open-source REST API and dataset of 747 historical geopolitical entities spanning 6,500 years (4500 BCE to 2024), with coordinates, GeoJSON boundaries (93% real polygon coverage), confidence scores, 2,200+ academic sources, and 2,000+ documented territory changes. The dataset is consumed by AI agents via a public REST API and an official MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. AtlasPI bridges the gap between fragmented geographic primary sources (Natural Earth, aourednik/historical-basemaps, Wikidata) and the structured, contextualized format required for automated historical reasoning. It documents conquest, displacement, and contested borders with an explicit ethical framework (ETHICS-001..005) covering transparency of uncertainty, multi-version borders, and no geographic/cultural bias.

Live service: https://atlaspi.cra-srl.com

MCP server: https://pypi.org/project/atlaspi-mcp/
